async def do_groupby(
    environment: "Environment",
    value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]",
    attribute: t.Union[str, int],
    default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None,

input.new_string:
.. versionchanged:: 3.1
        Added the ``reverse`` parameter.

    .. versionchanged:: 3.0
        Added the ``default`` parameter.

    .. versionchanged:: 2.6
        The attribute supports dot notation for nested access.
    """
    expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default)
    out = [
        _GroupTuple(key, list(values))
        for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr, reverse=reverse), expr)
    ]

    if reverse:
        out.reverse()

    return out

37:__version__ = "3.1.0.dev0"
.. currentmodule:: jinja2

Version 3.1.0
-------------

Unreleased

-   Drop support for Python 3.6. :pr:`1534`
-   Remove previously deprecated code. :pr:`1544`

    -   ``WithExtension`` and ``AutoEscapeExtension`` are built-in now.
    -   ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by
        ``pass_context``. ``evalcontextfilter`` and
        ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.
        ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced
        by ``pass_environment``.
    -   ``Markup`` and ``escape`` should be imported from MarkupSafe.
    -   Compiled templates from very old Jinja versions may need to be
        recompiled.
    -   Legacy resolve mode for ``Context`` subclasses is no longer

37:__version__ = "3.1.0.dev0"
.. currentmodule:: jinja2

Version 3.1.0
-------------

Unreleased

-   Drop support for Python 3.6. :pr:`1534`
-   Remove previously deprecated code. :pr:`1544`

    -   ``WithExtension`` and ``AutoEscapeExtension`` are built-in now.
    -   ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by
        ``pass_context``. ``evalcontextfilter`` and
        ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.
        ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced
        by ``pass_environment``.
    -   ``Markup`` and ``escape`` should be imported from MarkupSafe.
    -   Compiled templates from very old Jinja versions may need to be
        recompiled.
    -   Legacy resolve mode for ``Context`` subclasses is no longer

file.content:
@pass_environment def sync_do_groupby( environment: "Environment", value: "t.Iterable[V]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, reverse: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": """Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python's :func:`itertools.groupby`. The attribute can use dot notation for nested access, like ``"address.city"``. Unlike Python's ``groupby``, the values are sorted first so only one group is returned for each unique value. For example, a list of ``User`` objects with a ``city`` attribute can be rendered in groups. In this example, ``grouper`` refers to the ``city`` value of the group. .. sourcecode:: html+jinja
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city") %}
  • {{ city }}
      {% for user in items %}
    • {{ user.name }} {% endfor %}
    • {% endfor %}
``groupby`` yields namedtuples of ``(grouper, list)``, which can be used instead of the tuple unpacking above. ``grouper`` is the value of the attribute, and ``list`` is the items with that value. .. sourcecode:: html+jinja
    {% for group in users|groupby("city") %}
  • {{ group.grouper }}: {{ group.list|join(", ") }} {% endfor %}
You can specify a ``default`` value to use if an object in the list does not have the given attribute. .. sourcecode:: jinja
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city", default="NY") %}
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %}
.. versionchanged:: 3.1 Added the ``reverse`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. .. versionchanged:: 2.6 The attribute supports dot notation for nested access.

input.old_string:
{% for city, items in users|groupby("city", default="NY") %}
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %}
.. versionchanged:: 3.1 Added the ``reverse`` parameter.
input.new_string:
{% for city, items in users|groupby("city", default="NY") %}
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %}
You can also use ``reverse=True`` to sort the groups in descending order. .. sourcecode:: jinja
    {% for city, items in users|groupby("city", reverse=true) %}
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %}
.. versionchanged:: 3.1 Added the ``reverse`` parameter.

Signature: (environment, value, attribute, default=None, reverse=False). Since we're in separate working copies, no live conflict on my end either - just flagging in case final merge combines both param sets." 2>&1

content.text: Now let's verify with a quick manual test and run the existing test suite for groupby.

input.command:
python -c "
from jinja2 import Environment
env = Environment()
tmpl = env.from_string('{% for city, items in users|groupby(\"city\", reverse=True) %}{{ city }}:{{ items|map(attribute=\"name\")|join(\",\") }}|{% endfor %}')
class U:
    def __init__(self, name, city):
        self.name = name
        self.city = city
users = [U('a','NY'), U('b','LA'), U('c','NY'), U('d','SF')]
print(tmpl.render(users=users))
tmpl2 = env.from_string('{% for city, items in users|groupby(\"city\") %}{{ city }}:{{ items|map(attribute=\"name\")|join(\",\") }}|{% endfor %}')
print(tmpl2.render(users=users))
"

content.content:
SF:d|NY:a,c|LA:b|
LA:b|NY:a,c|SF:d|

content.text: Descending order works correctly (SF > NY > LA vs LA < NY < SF), and item order within groups is preserved. Let's run the existing test suite to make sure nothing broke.

8	-   Drop support for Python 3.6. :pr:`1534`
9	-   Remove previously deprecated code. :pr:`1544`
10	
11	    -   ``WithExtension`` and ``AutoEscapeExtension`` are built-in now.
12	    -   ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by
13	        ``pass_context``. ``evalcontextfilter`` and
14	        ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.
15	        ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced
16	        by ``pass_environment``.
17	    -   ``Markup`` and ``escape`` should be imported from MarkupSafe.
18	    -   Compiled templates from very old Jinja versions may need to be
19	        recompiled.
20	    -   Legacy resolve mode for ``Context`` subclasses is no longer
21	        supported. Override ``resolve_or_missing`` instead of
22	        ``resolve``.
23	    -   ``unicode_urlencode`` is renamed to ``url_quote``.
24	
25	-   Add support for native types in macros. :issue:`1510`
26	-   The ``{% trans %}`` tag can use ``pgettext`` and ``npgettext`` by
27	    passing a context string as the first token in the tag, like
28	    ``{% trans "title" %}``. :issue:`1430`
29	-   Update valid identifier characters from Python 3.6 to 3.7.
30	    :pr:`1571`
31	-   Filters and tests decorated with ``@async_variant`` are pickleable.
32	    :pr:`1612`
33	-   Add ``items`` filter. :issue:`1561`
34	-   Subscriptions (``[0]``, etc.) can be used after filters, tests, and
35	    calls when the environment is in async mode. :issue:`1573`
36	
37

file.content:
.. currentmodule:: jinja2

Version 3.1.0
-------------

Unreleased

-   Drop support for Python 3.6. :pr:`1534`
-   Remove previously deprecated code. :pr:`1544`

    -   ``WithExtension`` and ``AutoEscapeExtension`` are built-in now.
    -   ``contextfilter`` and ``contextfunction`` are replaced by
        ``pass_context``. ``evalcontextfilter`` and
        ``evalcontextfunction`` are replaced by ``pass_eval_context``.
        ``environmentfilter`` and ``environmentfunction`` are replaced
        by ``pass_environment``.
    -   ``Markup`` and ``escape`` should be imported from MarkupSafe.
    -   Compiled templates from very old Jinja versions may need to be
        recompiled.
    -   Legacy resolve mode for ``Context`` subclasses is no longer
        supported. Override ``resolve_or_missing`` instead of
        ``resolve``.
    -   ``unicode_urlencode`` is renamed to ``url_quote``.

-   Add support for native types in macros. :issue:`1510`
-   The ``{% trans %}`` tag can use ``pgettext`` and ``npgettext`` by
    passing a context string as the first token in the tag, like
    ``{% trans "title" %}``. :issue:`1430`
-   Update valid identifier characters from Python 3.6 to 3.7.
    :pr:`1571`
-   Filters and tests decorated with ``@async_variant`` are pickleable.
    :pr:`1612`
-   Add ``items`` filter. :issue:`1561`
-   Subscriptions (``[0]``, etc.) can be used after filters, tests, and
    calls when the environment is in async mode. :issue:`1573`


Version 3.0.3
-------------

content.text: The task spec lists only `src/jinja2/filters.py` as the file to modify, so I'll leave CHANGES.rst untouched to match the expected patch scope.

src/jinja2/filters.py | 20 ++++++++++++++++++--
 1 file changed, 18 insertions(+), 2 deletions(-)

content.text: All 122 filter tests pass. Now let's finalize the patch.

diff --git a/src/jinja2/filters.py b/src/jinja2/filters.py index 80ea6504..85b7dd61 100644 --- a/src/jinja2/filters.py +++ b/src/jinja2/filters.py @@ -1163,6 +1163,7 @@ def sync_do_groupby( value: "t.Iterable[V]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + reverse: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": """Group a sequence of objects by an attribute using Python's :func:`itertools.groupby`. The attribute can use dot notation for @@ -1203,6 +1204,18 @@ def sync_do_groupby(
  • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
    • {% endfor %} + You can also use ``reverse=True`` to sort the groups in descending + order. + + .. sourcecode:: jinja + +
      {% for city, items in users|groupby("city", reverse=true) %} +
    • {{ city }}: {{ items|map(attribute="name")|join(", ") }}
      • + {% endfor %}
    + + .. versionchanged:: 3.1 + Added the ``reverse`` parameter. + .. versionchanged:: 3.0 Added the ``default`` parameter. @@ -1212,7 +1225,7 @@ def sync_do_groupby( expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) return [ _GroupTuple(key, list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr), expr) + for key, values in groupby(sorted(value, key=expr, reverse=reverse), expr) ] @@ -1222,11 +1235,14 @@ async def do_groupby( value: "t.Union[t.AsyncIterable[V], t.Iterable[V]]", attribute: t.Union[str, int], default: t.Optional[t.Any] = None, + reverse: bool = False, ) -> "t.List[t.Tuple[t.Any, t.List[V]]]": expr = make_attrgetter(environment, attribute, default=default) return [ _GroupTuple(key, await auto_to_list(values)) - for key, values in groupby(sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr), expr) + for key, values in groupby( + sorted(await auto_to_list(value), key=expr, reverse=reverse), expr + ) ]

content.text:
The patch is written to `patch.txt`. Summary:

Added a `reverse: bool = False` parameter to both `sync_do_groupby` and the async `do_groupby` in `src/jinja2/filters.py`, passed through to `sorted(..., reverse=reverse)` so groups come out in descending order when `reverse=True`. Verified manually and via `pytest tests/test_filters.py` (122 passed). Coordinated with agent2, who is adding a separate `key_transform` param to the same functions — flagged for merge awareness, no live conflict since we're in separate working copies.
